The GOP's voter suppression agenda should scare us all:
-> In Texas, top elections officials tried to purge voters from the rolls based on false, misinformed data
-> In Georgia, many voters waited in line for up to 3 hours due to a lack of polling locations and broken machines
-> In Kansas, polling places were moved to locations not accessible by public transportation
Republicans on the House Oversight Committee don't want these actions to be investigated -- they want to **COVER UP** this up and disenfranchise voters (especially voters of color) with 2020 around the corner.
[**The Voter Protection Projection is ready to defeat the GOP at the ballot box -- Be one of the 500 donors we need TODAY to help defend voting rights and stop the GOP 's discriminatory agenda:**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0782/72412298/44b196b2/6100b096/3029235176/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vpp_monthly_votingrights?refcode=VPP_EM_FR_190416_GOPvotersuppression_U1_X1&amount=25&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+The+Voter+Protection+Projection+is+ready&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_FR_190416_GOPvotersuppression_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_FR_190416_GOPvotersuppression_U1_X1")

body:
|
#
Kamala says it all the time: _“We know these are dark times that require us to fight and march and resist – but as we are fighting,**let’s be joyful warriors**. Let us understand that we are not fighting against something, we are fighting for something.”_ **[Get your FREE “Joyful Warrior” sticker while there’s still time — supplies are limited and they’re going FAST!](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190415-joyful-warrior-sticker/?refcode=ema190416-B&source=ema190416-B&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1&akid=4363%2E5755789%2Ek0SCT5)** | | [ GET YOUR FREE STICKER](https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190415-joyful-warrior-sticker/?refcode=ema190416-B&source=ema190416-B&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=2&akid=4363%2E5755789%2Ek0SCT5)
---
Here’s what we’re fighting for in this campaign:
Raising teacher salaries.
Making health care a right with Medicare for All.
Cutting taxes for working families.
Protecting everyone’s civil rights.
Passing common-sense gun safety reforms and keeping our schools safe from mass shootings.
And so much more!

subject: I’ll get to the point
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
It's been a few weeks since you last heard from me.
| | Hi Peter,

It's been a few weeks since you last heard from me. I've been busy chugging away at our numbers from the first fundraising quarter -- thank you again for all of your help! -- and outlining a plan to put your grassroots contributions to their best use.

Your first-quarter donations to Abigail's grassroots campaign are making a huge difference for us already. Abigail isn't taking any money from corporate PACs, so every dollar that we've raised so far is a key indicator of the grassroots support we can expect down the road. **We 're already budgeting for the 2020 election, and, I can tell you now: It's going to be expensive.**

We have to make sure that we run an aggressive voter contact program that reaches every corner of the district. We need to make sure we can afford to get Abigail's message across in mailboxes, on TVs, and online. We need to hire an amazing staff and pay for a campaign office. It's incredibly expensive to run for Congress, Peter!

So, I bet you can guess why I'm reaching out today...

I'll get to the point: We're just over halfway through April, which means I need to make sure that we're on track to reach our end-of-month goal.

[**As of this morning, we 're just a bit shy of where we need to be. Peter, will you make a grassroots donation toward our goal so we can keep our operation running at full capacity? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/GcHArlRWXRook4hrCnXUJ9HHoog5qtmdYuGx9FAZpAY=/a3dUjQhLFR10_k_VFA3vj1J84k1FtS_GRtCD6oamUWI=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.17_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)

I'm sure you haven't forgotten how close Abigail's election was last year -- it came down to just 6,700 votes! We have every reason to expect that we'll be in an incredibly close election like that again in 2020.

And, if we allow dark money groups and SuperPACs to get the upper hand now, we'll be playing catch-up until Election Day.

[**Peter -- we need your help by tonight. Can you chip in $25, or whatever works for you, so we can hit our fundraising target? I wouldn 't be reaching out unless it was necessary. >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/GcHArlRWXRook4hrCnXUJ9HHoog5qtmdYuGx9FAZpAY=/a3dUjQhLFR10_k_VFA3vj1J84k1FtS_GRtCD6oamUWI=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.17_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/GcHArlRWXRook4hrCnXUJ9HHoog5qtmdYuGx9FAZpAY=/a3dUjQhLFR10_k_VFA3vj1J84k1FtS_GRtCD6oamUWI=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.17_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)

subject: Can you give us a moment to explain why this is so important?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Every single contribution keeps building momentum for Elizabeth's big ideas. That includes this week's new proposal to fight climate change and protect our public lands. | | |
---
We'll level with you:

We're about to ask you to chip in $3 or whatever you can spare today. But before we get to that, will you give us a moment to explain why your donation matters -- especially right now?

Every single contribution keeps building momentum for Elizabeth's big ideas. That includes this week's new proposal to fight climate change and protect our public lands -- and her plans to make sure giant corporations, ultra-millionaires, and billionaires pay their fair share in taxes.

Every new grassroots donor like you is another member of our movement.

And the more people who chip in to own a piece of our campaign, the harder it will be for powerful special interests to pretend that people don't want big, structural change.

With your support, our grassroots movement will continue to grow -- without taking any big checks from wealthy donors at private fundraising events, and with no support from PACs or federal lobbyists.

And chipping in right now, at this early stage, helps lay a strong foundation for the rest of the campaign -- and makes the long-term investments in organizing we need to win possible. That's why this is so important.

**[So we're asking: Can you make your first contribution to our campaign right now? $3, or whatever you can afford, would go a long way toward building a winning grassroots campaign.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fc89/45c30650/b6e88aab/66977026/2523579600/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNDE3ZW0xLXYxczMifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190417em1-v1s3")**

Elizabeth has a plan -- actually, many plans! -- to make our economy and our government work for everyone. And she's ready to help lead this fight.

**But first, we need to build a powerful grassroots movement to get it done. And that comes down to people like you.**

Thanks,

Team Warren

subject: Strong Jobs Market Spurs Bigger Salary Offers
body:
"Americans still think plenty of jobs are available and companies are offering better pay as they compete for a shrinking pool of available labor." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e24dd4d609&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Strong Jobs Market Spurs Bigger Salary Offers**

“Americans still think plenty of jobs are available and companies are offering better pay as they compete for a shrinking pool of available labor, new study by the New York Federal Reserve shows,” Jeffry Bartash reports in _MarketWatch_.

“The Fed study is the latest proof the jobs market continues to sizzle. The rate of layoffs each week — known as jobless claims — fell below 200,000 earlier this month for the first time since 1969. The economy also added almost 200,000 new jobs in March after a feeble 33,000 gain in February, keeping the unemployment rate at 3.8%.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4b2e55a805&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Obama used to stir Americans with his powerful calls for national unity. It looks like he has, after all these years, finally forged agreement among leaders in both parties that his health care law made things worse and should be replaced. Bipartisanship blooms in Washington,” Scott Jennings writes in the [Courier-Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=84d887397e&e=3ee4985eea). “Voters in both parties know they were duped by Obama’s health care promises. Private insurance customers saw their premiums and deductibles jump to pay for someone else’s health care, but not their own.”
---
|
---
| “How’s this for unexpected irony: As New York pols move to choke their own economy by nixing new natural-gas supplies, President Trump is racing to the rescue,” the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4dc689f58c&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “Trump last week signed two executive orders to help ease the way for new oil and gas pipelines and other energy projects. One, seemingly aimed squarely at New York, requires the Environmental Protection Agency to look for ways to make it harder for states to use the Clean Water Act to block pipelines”—a common tactic of Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D-NY), the editors add.
---
|
---
| “An Arizona mayor on Tuesday declared an emergency in the city he represents due to what he said was the strain caused by the incoming flow of migrants,” Elizabeth Zwirz reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0a48f0627c&e=3ee4985eea). “Yuma Mayor Douglas Nicholls shared the news on Facebook, explaining that he ‘proclaimed a local emergency’ in the city ‘due to the migrant family releases overwhelming the local shelter system.’”
---
|

---

name: Andrew Yang
subject: The Case for the Freedom Dividend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend -- In my last email, I outlined why I decided to run—we are facing existential problems in this country, and we need big solutions to solve them. The biggest solution is what I am most known for proposing—The Freedom Dividend, $1,000 a month for every American adult, free and clear, no strings attached. To some, this might sound far-fetched or too good to be true. The good news is that, as you can imagine, I’ve done my homework and run the numbers, and I wanted to break down for you why the Freedom Dividend is our best way forward:
1. **The Freedom Dividend is deeply American.****
****
**At the founding of our country, Thomas Paine supported a Universal Basic Income. Since then, Milton Friedman, Martin Luther King, Jr., Richard Nixon, and, more recently, Barack Obama, Elon Musk, and Tony Robbins have all been in support of the idea. In fact, a basic income passed the House of Representatives—twice—in 1971, before stalling in the Senate because the Democrats wanted a higher income. Additionally, Alaska has had a universal income for nearly 40 years, where residents receive between $1,000 to $2,000 a year—and it’s wildly popular in a conservative state.

2. **We can afford it.****
****
**Our economy has grown to $20 trillion, up $5 trillion in the last 12 years alone. By creating a Value Added Tax at half the European level, we could easily pay for this. No shareholders ever complain when a company declares a dividend. This is a dividend for the American people, as the shareholders of our prosperous nation.

3. **The Freedom Dividend would create millions of jobs.****
****
**With everyone in your community getting $1,000 a month, people would have more money to spend at local businesses, and these businesses would then create thousands of new jobs. We know what people would spend this money on—car repairs, tutoring for their kids, health care bills, the occasional night out, home repairs. This would rebuild main streets across the country, creating economic opportunity outside of a few cities on the coasts and giving our children a reason to stay. The Freedom Dividend would be the greatest catalyst for entrepreneurship in the history of the world.

4. **It would help marginalized communities.****
****
**Women do the majority of unpaid labor in this country while also failing to receive equal pay for equal work at many institutions. Because of this, they often lack the economic freedom to walk away from abusive relationships or workplaces. Black families have a median net worth under $18k; Hispanic families have a median net worth of slightly over $20k. Members of these communities also often lack access to family savings, financial institutions, and financial education because of a lack of availability in their areas or outright exclusion. In the LGBTQ+ community, healthcare costs for their unique needs often come out of pocket. Additionally, many lose access to family support when they reveal their gender identity or sexual orientation, and this group is overrepresented in foster homes and homeless shelters. For all these Americans, the Freedom Dividend could make a life-saving difference or allow them to create economic opportunities for their families and communities. They could escape bad situations, pay down debt, and start to save and invest in their futures.

5. **It would eliminate the financial insecurity that has swept this country.****
****
**40% of Americans cannot afford an unexpected $400 bill right now, and millions of Americans who work full-time—often at two jobs—can’t make ends meet. It is impossible to expect someone struggling to survive today to worry about our greatest threats such as climate change or AI. $1,000 a month would transform the lives and mentalities of millions of Americans. It would give everyone a secure sense of the future, and reverse the mindset of scarcity that has overcome so many of our friends, replacing it with a mindset of abundance and possibility.

The Freedom Dividend will show that we believe in people, by investing in people. [**If you believe the case for the Freedom Dividend should reach the American people, please donate just $10 to the campaign today. $10 puts your name on the list and tells the democratic party you want to see me on the debate stage. We need to get 200,000 donors by this summer to make sure we’re the story of the debates.**](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztii6erBGLB5rcI5FHmBMT9fCQQSgOwqm_gQYw_I-a_dA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=04172019_onboard2&n=1) Your fellow Patriot, -Andrew

name: James Mackler
subject: Voter suppression
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Tennessee has one of the lowest voter registration and turnout rates in the country.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa28c/18d6a294/4ade1687/26d6a0f6/1973971686/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | It's hard to believe--Tennessee has one of the lowest voter registration and turnout rates in the country. We should be doing everything in our power to encourage more Tennesseans to vote and make their voices heard. Our democracy would be stronger for it. Unfortunately, the gerrymandered super-majority in the state legislature and our appointed Secretary of State have other ideas. They're pushing legislation making it even harder for voters to participate. | | _CNN_ : Tennessee wants to make it harder to hold voter registration drives _" A bill currently making its way through the Tennessee legislature would impose new restrictions on groups that hold voter registration drives and subject them to potential jail time and massive fines."_
---
There isn't another state that punishes individuals or civic groups for mistakes made on voter registration applications. The folks pushing this bill cite how much it costs to process voter registration forms--a problem that could disappear, at less cost to taxpayers, almost immediately by automatically registering citizens to vote.
But encouraging greater civic participation and strengthening our democracy isn't the aim of this bill or its authors. Fear of all these new penalties could discourage groups--many run by volunteers--from registering new voters.
Legislation like this is for one purpose only: suppressing the vote. I won't stand for it. [**_Add your name to join me in opposing this bill._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa28c/18d6a294/4ade1687/26d6a0f7/1973971686/VEsE/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190417")
| | [DEFEND VOTING RIGHTS >>](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa28c/18d6a294/4ade1687/26d6a0f7/1973971686/VEsF/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190417")
---
Thank you,
James

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I want you to know this
body:
They are doing this not because they fear Bernie Sanders. They fear all of us together. We have 48 hours to prove them right.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1079%2E150750%2EUWM-r4)

Do you know how powerful you are?
You have some of the wealthiest members of the political establishment hiding behind closed doors — with their canapés — plotting how to defeat our political revolution.
You have some of the biggest players in Washington frantically putting their heads together for how to stop us.
You have career political operatives begging the financial elite for money to start new efforts to derail our movement.
You have some of these same people telling the _New York Times_ that we need to “fall in line.”
They are doing this not because they fear Bernie Sanders. They fear all of us together.
**They know what we know: It’s not about me. It’s about us.**
If there is one thing that the political and financial elite of this country understand, it is money in politics. And they don’t like that more than one million contributions have already brought our campaign this far.
So let’s make sure we respond in a way they are sure to understand, with an emergency 48-hour fundraising drive for our campaign.
[**Please make a $3 contribution to our emergency 48-hour fundraising drive to send an unmistakable message to the establishment about the power of our political revolution.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10999?refcode=em190417-1-15&t=26&akid=1079%2E150750%2EUWM-r4)
Our power comes from a simple, timeless truth: when people come together, there is nothing we cannot accomplish.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

name: Dick Durbin
subject: Add your name to support the REMEDY Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
**[I'm fighting in Congress to rein in Big Pharma and lower prescription drug prices for all Americans. Add your name to support the REMEDY Act now.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4d43/42ab3cf4/7c0aa25b/135d66d7/1017326772/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/add-your-name-to-support-the-remedy-act?source=em190417&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
Peter,
Prescription drug costs are out of control. It's time for urgent action to rein in the abusive practices of Big Pharma to ensure these manufacturers can't rig the system to keep more affordable generic drug alternatives out of the hands of American patients and consumers.
That's why I introduced the REMEDY Act, a bipartisan bill that aims to break up pharmaceutical monopolies, increase competition, and lower drug prices for patients across the country.
**[Congress must take urgent action on the soaring costs of life-saving prescription drugs. Add your name to support the REMEDY Act now to decrease prices for everyone.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4d43/42ab3cf4/7c0aa25b/135d66d7/1017326772/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/add-your-name-to-support-the-remedy-act?source=em190417&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
Big Pharma has figured out how to game the system: if they file new patents for drugs that are already available, they can extend their monopolies and drastically increase their profits.
Standing up to Big Pharma and ensuring patients can access more affordable generic drugs is not a partisan issue, which is why I'm working with Republican Senator Bill Cassidy on this bill. Patients paying these exorbitant prices don't care about our political persuasions -- they just need us to work together and get this done so they can afford their life-saving medications.
**[Americans have had enough of skyrocketing drug prices. Thankfully, we have a bipartisan solution to this growing crisis. Sign on now to support the REMEDY Act.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4d43/42ab3cf4/7c0aa25b/135d66d7/1017326772/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/add-your-name-to-support-the-remedy-act?source=em190417&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
My best,
Dick Durbin
**[Add My Name](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4d43/42ab3cf4/7c0aa25b/135d66d7/1017326772/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/add-your-name-to-support-the-remedy-act?source=em190417&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**

If you haven’t donated yet to help us hit out $75,000 pre-primary goal, you can use this link to do it now: [**_https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ahe-pre-primary-fund_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/6134056/55163255/-230771582?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MjYxNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmMzYzgzNS02NTYxLWU5MTEtYjQ5ZS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ayxM27ZFWZxqi3BqbffBZv19gBU4YpGq22K0gkGMxMU=&refcode=AE_EM_FR_30DO_190417_2_2&amount=25&refcode2=4bc3c835-6561-e911-b49e-281878391efb&recurring=auto)

**_\---Begin forwarded message from Adam Edelen---_**

Hi team,

This is a huge week for our campaign—maybe one of the biggest weeks yet actually. This Sunday we’re filing our 30-day pre-primary finance report. These numbers will determine how competitive we are and how seriously our opponents, the pundits, and the voters will take this campaign.

We need to post strong numbers to prove that even though we’re an underdog campaign that doesn’t take a dime from corporate PACs, we still have a strong base of support and we’re prepared to win this thing.

body:
This can change the world. | | |
# I need your help.
---
| | My name is David Oks, and I'm managing the Gravel 2020 campaign. A little about me: I'm a senior in high school. My family is Argentine-American, and I was raised by a single mother, a social worker. I see myself as a leftist in the vein of John Dewey, John Rawls, and Richard Rorty.

This campaign has the potential to change this country. Bringing Sen. Gravel to the debate stage would bring new, radical ideas to public attention. It would provide cover for the anti-war leftists in the field. And it would allow Sen. Gravel to say what the other leftist candidates, burdened by a desire to remain "electable," are unwilling to say.

Right now, we've hit a quarter of the donors we need to get to 65,000 and qualify for the debates. Our movement is doing well; in the new Emerson poll, we beat Kirsten Gillibrand! But we need to get Mike to the debates. This is an opportunity—a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity—to change this country.

**If each of you were to just get three people you know to donate, we'd qualify today.**

Here is what I ask: get 10 people you know to throw in a dollar. I know, I know, it might be weird asking your spouse or partner to donate. But we're trying to change the world. We're trying to save this country. We need you to do everything you can to help get us 65,000 unique donors.

Here are a few things you can do:
1. Forward this email to 25 people you know.
2. Get your partner to donate $1.
3. Get your parents to donate $1.
4. Talk to your friends about donating $1. Be an evangelist.

I really am eternally grateful for this. Let me know if you're ever visiting Ardsley, NY; and if you'd like to speak to me about anything, just email [david@mikegravel.org](mailto:david@mikegravel.org?subject=Hi%20David) and I'll get back to you shortly. Please, please do this: I beg it of you.

[](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=4bdec4b0d9&e=85f7472d66)

David Oks

Team --
Recent Affordable Care Act headlines are incredibly alarming. Take this one, from the Washington Post: _" Trump administration asks court to completely invalidate Obama's Affordable Care Act."_
You're reading this correctly: **The Trump administration is trying to fully repeal the Affordable Care Act in its entirety in federal court.**
The stakes are extremely high, everyone, and we need all hands on deck.** [Will you please sign our petition calling on the Trump administration to stop attacking health care?](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc292/2a380d31/38ed5bda/f116c1/3895333710/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/protect-the-ACA/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2019_protect-the-aca-up%3Frefcode%3De20190417_acaja_all")**
If Trump is successful, it could mean that people with pre-existing conditions will lose their badly-needed protections. But it's not just people with pre-existing conditions who would be vulnerable -- millions of Americans could lose their coverage altogether.
Look, Senator Hassan believes that everyone deserves access to the care they need to get and stay healthy. And, she believes that it should be affordable. That's why she's been fighting day in and day out to protect these fundamental rights -- and to make sure that no one ends up with a surprise medical bill in the process.
[**Senator Hassan needs to know that you 're with her in this fight, so will you please add your name to our petition and call on President Trump to stop attacking health care?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc292/2a380d31/38ed5bda/f116c1/3895333710/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/protect-the-ACA/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2019_protect-the-aca-up%3Frefcode%3De20190417_acaja_all")
Thank you for speaking up,
Team Maggie

Take this one, from the Washington Post: " Trump administration asks court to completely invalidate Obama's Affordable Care Act." You're reading this correctly: The Trump administration is trying to fully repeal the Affordable Care Act in its entirety in federal court. The stakes are extremely high, everyone, and we need all hands on deck. "" If Trump is successful, it could mean that people with pre-existing conditions will lose their badly-needed protections. But it's not just people with pre-existing conditions who would be vulnerable -- millions of Americans could lose their coverage altogether. Look, Senator Hassan believes that everyone deserves access to the care they need to get and stay healthy. And, she believes that it should be affordable. That's why she's been fighting day in and day out to protect these fundamental rights -- and to make sure that no one ends up with a surprise medical bill in the process. "" Thank you for speaking up, Team Maggie ""

subject: Lifting up poor communities—the conservative way
body:
These past few weeks, millions of Americans filed their taxes and discovered that President Donald J. Trump delivered on his promise. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a9b51f9207&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 17, 2019
---
| | |
# **Lifting up poor communities—the conservative way**

**These past few weeks,** millions of Americans filed their taxes and discovered that President Donald J. Trump delivered on his promise to lower their tax bills for 2018 and beyond. But tax reform was a conservative policy breakthrough for more reasons than meet the eye on first glance.

**Today, President Trump** brought State and local leaders from both parties together at the White House for a conference on “Opportunity Zones”—a signature piece of his 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act. Rather than spending more money on yet another government program or Washington bureaucracy, Opportunity Zones offer tax incentives for long-term investment in more than 8,760 low-income communities throughout America.

**“Across the country,** our tax cuts have kicked off a race to invest in Opportunity Zones, beyond anything that anyone in this room even thought,” President Trump said today.

**Opportunity Zones** are a powerful vehicle for bringing economic growth and job creation to the American communities that need them the most. The census tracts eligible for the program were chosen by Governors, then certified by the U.S. Department of the Treasury. All told, the program is anticipated to spur $100 billion in private capital investment.

**“Nearly 35 million people** live in Opportunity Zones . . . and will now have a pathway to financial success due to a single stroke of a pen. I want to thank the President for that,” Housing and Urban Development Secretary Ben Carson said today.

**The core of President Trump’s** economic agenda is lifting up the forgotten men and women of America. Through a mix of traditional Republican policies, such as tax cuts and deregulation, along with tough negotiating on trade deals and strong border security, wages for blue-collar workers are taking off even more quickly than they are for higher-income Americans. That’s a huge win toward a fairer, sturdier economy.

**Opportunity Zones** build on that success, applying conservative principles to help the most distressed communities across our country. For years, liberal politicians have given lip service to building up these places, only to spend more taxpayer money with little to show for it. Once again, President Trump is shaking up the status quo by having Washington put American citizens before its own self-interest.

[President Trump is lifting up American communities that have been left behind](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1c785fcded&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch** : [Revitalization doesn’t have a color or a party, Scott Turner says](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bb9a6b1304&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **When you live near the border, the crisis is real**

**From the cozy** confines of the Beltway, Congressional Democrats continue to tell Americans that the border emergency is nothing more than a “manufactured crisis.” But that’s vintage Washington: a tone-deaf lecture circuit that ignores the very real problems people face in their own backyards.

**Just yesterday,** Mayor Douglas Nicholls of Yuma, Arizona, [declared a local emergency](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b15c2b1f37&e=3ee4985eea) in his city caused by the ongoing surge of illegal immigration. His community is quickly becoming overwhelmed, he explained, because detainees are “being released into the community faster than they are departing, and shelters and the staff to run them are at max capacity.”

**Yuma is not the only place** facing a crisis—far from it. Just last week, U.S. Border Patrol reported that the El Paso Police Department in Texas is “now having to use their resources to help with the migrants that enter into the city illegally,” Christina Aguayo reports for [KVIA](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b34568683d&e=3ee4985eea).

[In case you missed it: “This is a crisis by any measure,” says former Obama official.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e7ce75862d&e=3ee4985eea)

# Previous Blog Posts

### [The Dems' First 100 Days](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-d/)
Well, it's been 100 days now since Nancy Pelosi was sworn in again as Speaker, and Democrats took over the U.S. House of Representatives. So how much have they been able to accomplish?Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Where the Democrats Want to Take Us](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-u/)
If you can't win fair and square, change the rules, rig things more to your liking. That's what Washington Democrats are trying to do. This week I'm on the blog talking about the Democrats' first bill, H.R. 1, and what exactly is in it.Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-b/)
## [Exoneration](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jifutkd-kujkulhluu-n/)
We've had a couple of days for it to sink in now. Special Counsel Robert Mueller finally delivered his verdict on alleged collusion between the Trump campaign and the Russians. And Mueller says - no collusion. So, now it's time to put all this behind us, and move on to solving real issues facing our country.Click the link below to read this week's blog post on where I think we go from here.
